title: Pruning and Distillation
concept: Pruning removes unimportant weights, while Knowledge Distillation transfers knowledge from a large teacher model to a smaller student model.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main difference between PTQ and QAT?

options.text: PTQ happens after training; QAT happens during training
PTQ is for GPUs; QAT is for CPUs
PTQ increases precision; QAT decreases precision

exercises.explain: PTQ is a fast post-training conversion, whereas QAT integrates rounding constraints during model training.

cards.title: Trim the fat, teach the youth
cards.body: In a neural network, many weight connections are close to zero and contribute almost nothing to the final prediction. We can cut them out (pruning). We can also have a massive model teach a tiny model its secrets (distillation). Both techniques make edge AI incredibly small!

cards.title: What are Pruning & Distillation?
cards.body: Pruning sets unimportant model weights (often based on low magnitude) to zero. Knowledge Distillation trains a compact student model to match the probability distributions (soft labels) of a large teacher model.

cards.title: Gardening and school tutoring
cards.body: Pruning is like trimming dead branches off a bush so it uses less water (resources) but keeps its shape. Knowledge distillation is like a university professor tutoring a high-school student, giving them the key summaries instead of making them read 1,000 textbooks.

cards.title: ResNet to MobileNet distillation
cards.body: A team trains a heavy ResNet-152 model on a custom dataset. They use it as a teacher to train a lightweight MobileNet-V2 student. The student achieves 95% of the teacher's accuracy but is 10x faster.

cards.title: Pruning needs hardware support to run faster
cards.body: You might think setting weights to zero automatically speeds up any CPU. In reality, unless you use structured pruning (removing entire channels) or have specialized hardware that skips zero-multiplications, a sparse model still takes the same time to execute.

left.text:
Removes weak connections
Sets small weights to zero
Shrinks weight sparse count
Needs sparse hardware support

right.text:
Teacher guides student
Student mimics teacher outputs
Compact model architecture
Runs fast on standard chips

exercises.prompt: What is the process of setting low-value weights in a neural network to zero called?
exercises.explain: Pruning trims away unimportant weights, creating a sparse network.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In knowledge distillation, the student model is typically much larger and slower than the teacher model.
exercises.explain: False. The teacher is the large, slow, accurate model; the student is the small, fast model trained to mimic it.

exercises.sentence: Knowledge distillation uses the teacher's ___ labels (class probabilities) to guide the student model.
exercises.explain: Soft labels (probabilities like 0.7 cat, 0.2 dog) contain richer information about what the model learns than hard labels (cat).

exercises.prompt: Which type of pruning is easiest for standard CPUs to accelerate without specialized hardware?

options.text: Structured pruning (removing entire channels or neurons)

exercises.explain: Removing entire channels (structured pruning) changes matrix dimensions, allowing standard hardware to compute smaller operations directly.

exercises.prompt: You have a massive transformer-based vision model that is too large to fit on a smartphone. You want to create a lightweight version that still mimics the complex decision-making of the original. What compression workflow should you use?

options.text: Use the massive model as a teacher to perform knowledge distillation into a small MobileNet student, then quantize the student to INT8.
Double the layers of the student model to make it more complex.
Run unstructured pruning on the massive model and deploy it without quantization.
Train the massive model on half the dataset to shrink its capacity.

exercises.explain: Combining knowledge distillation (to shrink model architecture) with quantization (to shrink parameter bits) is an industry-standard pipeline for exporting models to mobile devices.
